rules.title: No Hardcoded Secrets
rules.description: Source code must not contain hardcoded secrets, API keys, tokens, passwords, or credentials.

rules.title: No Wildcard CORS with Credentials
rules.description: CORS configuration must not combine allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True.

rules.title: No Hardcoded Localhost/Dev URLs
rules.description: Non-configuration source files must not contain hardcoded localhost or development environment URLs.

rules.title: Authentication on Protected Routes
rules.description: All non-public routes must have authentication enforcement (style is user-defined in standard).

rules.title: Authorization on Mutating Routes
rules.description: All mutating non-public routes (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) must have authorization checks (style is user-defined).

rules.title: Request Validation on Write Endpoints
rules.description: Write endpoints (POST/PUT/PATCH) must validate incoming payloads using the declared validation mechanism.

rules.title: Response Contracts on Data Endpoints
rules.description: Endpoints returning data must use an enforceable response contract (style is user-defined).

rules.title: Error Handling Coverage
rules.description: All endpoints must have error handling coverage through the declared strategy.

rules.title: No Debug/Testing Flags in Production
rules.description: Production code must not contain enabled debug flags, testing toggles, or development-mode markers.

rules.title: Timeout on Outbound HTTP Requests
rules.description: All outbound HTTP requests must specify explicit timeouts.

rules.title: No Blocking Calls in Async Functions
rules.description: Async functions must not call blocking I/O libraries that can freeze the event loop.

rules.title: No Bare/Swallowed Exceptions
rules.description: Exception handlers must not use bare except or silently swallow errors with pass.

rules.title: Ownership Checks on Resource Endpoints
rules.description: Endpoints accessing resources by ID must verify caller ownership or tenant scoping (mechanism is user-defined).

rules.title: Input Sanitization Before Risky Sinks
rules.description: User input must be sanitized before being passed to SQL, OS commands, template engines, or other risky sinks.

rules.title: No Sensitive Data in Responses
rules.description: Response schemas must not expose sensitive fields (passwords, tokens, secrets) without explicit redaction.
